title: Orthogonalized Synthetic Controls
abstract: arXiv:2507.00307v3 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: When conducting inference for the average treatment effect on the treated with a Synthetic Control Estimator, the vector of control weights is a nuisance parameter that is often constrained, high-dimensional, and may be only partially identified even when the average treatment effect on the treated is point-identified. All three of these features of a nuisance parameter can lead to failure of asymptotic normality for the estimate of the parameter of interest when using standard methods. I provide a new method that yields asymptotic normality for an estimate of average treatment effects, even when all three complications are present. This is accomplished by first estimating the control weights and any other nuisance parameters using a regularization penalty to achieve identification, and then estimating average treatment effects using moment conditions that are orthogonalized with respect to the nuisance parameters. translated_abstract: arXiv:2507.00307v3 公告类型：替换 摘要：在使用合成控制估计量对处理组的平均处理效应进行推断时，控制权重向量是一个常受约束、高维且即使处理组平均处理效应被点识别时也可能仅被部分识别的干扰参数。干扰参数的这三个特征可能导致使用标准方法时感兴趣的参数估计值渐进正态性失效。本文提出了一种新方法，即使在所有三个复杂情况同时存在时，也能使平均处理效应估计值满足渐进正态性。该方法首先通过使用正则化惩罚来估计控制权重及其他干扰参数以实现识别，然后利用与干扰参数正交化的矩条件来估计平均处理效应。此外，我进一步...
tldr: 本文提出了一种正交化合成控制方法，通过正则化惩罚识别干扰参数并利用正交化矩条件，解决了在干扰参数受约束、高维且部分识别时，处理组平均处理效应估计的渐进正态性问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes an orthogonalized synthetic control method that achieves asymptotic normality for average treatment effect estimates by using regularization penalty for nuisance parameter identification and orthogonalized moment conditions, addressing challenges when nuisance parameters are constrained, high-dimensional, and partially identified.
